name: Ochsenschwanzsuppe (Ox Tail Soup)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 4-quart Dutch Oven, brown the ox tails and onion in hot oil for several minutes.
2. Add water, salt, and peppercorns; bring to a simmer uncovered. Cook for about 2 hours.
3. Cover and continue simmering for an additional 3 hours.
4. Add parsley, carrots, celery, bay leaf, tomatoes, and thyme. Continue simmering for another 30 minutes or until vegetables are tender.
5. Strain the stock and refrigerate for at least one hour.
6. In a blender, puree the edible meat and vegetables. Set aside.
7. Remove fat from the top of the stock and reheat it.
8. In a large, dry frypan, brown flour over high heat until golden brown.
9. Cool slightly. Add butter or margarine and blend in.
10. Gradually add the strained stock and vegetables to the pan, stirring constantly.
11. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed.
12. Just before serving, add Madeira wine for added flavor.
